meta.name: CNC Machining Cost Calculator
meta.description: Industrial-grade cost estimation for CNC machining operations based on ISO 9001, Lean Manufacturing, and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Material Type
inputs.businessContext: Material selection directly impacts tool wear, cutting speed, and cost per part.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material selection directly impacts tool wear, cutting speed, and cost per part.

inputs.label: Part Weight (kg)
inputs.businessContext: Weight of the finished part, used to calculate raw material cost and scrap.

businessContext_i18n.en: Weight of the finished part, used to calculate raw material cost and scrap.

inputs.label: Stock Volume (cm³)
inputs.businessContext: Volume of raw material block before machining, determines material usage.

businessContext_i18n.en: Volume of raw material block before machining, determines material usage.

inputs.label: Machining Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Total cycle time per part including cutting, tool changes, and rapid moves.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total cycle time per part including cutting, tool changes, and rapid moves.

inputs.label: Setup Time (minutes)
inputs.businessContext: Time to fixture, align, and program the machine for the batch.

businessContext_i18n.en: Time to fixture, align, and program the machine for the batch.

inputs.label: Batch Size (parts)
inputs.businessContext: Number of identical parts produced in one production run.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of identical parts produced in one production run.

inputs.label: Machine Hourly Rate ($/hr)
inputs.businessContext: Fully burdened machine cost including depreciation, maintenance, and energy.

businessContext_i18n.en: Fully burdened machine cost including depreciation, maintenance, and energy.

inputs.label: Labor Hourly Rate ($/hr)
inputs.businessContext: Operator wage including overhead and benefits.

businessContext_i18n.en: Operator wage including overhead and benefits.

inputs.label: Tooling Cost per Part ($)
inputs.businessContext: Consumable tooling cost allocated per part (inserts, drills, end mills).

businessContext_i18n.en: Consumable tooling cost allocated per part (inserts, drills, end mills).

inputs.label: Overhead Percentage (%)
inputs.businessContext: Facility, quality, and administrative overhead as a percentage of direct costs.

businessContext_i18n.en: Facility, quality, and administrative overhead as a percentage of direct costs.

inputs.label: Scrap Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of parts that fail inspection and are scrapped.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of parts that fail inspection and are scrapped.

inputs.label: Material Cost per kg ($/kg)
inputs.businessContext: Price of raw material per kilogram, varies by material type.

businessContext_i18n.en: Price of raw material per kilogram, varies by material type.

inputs.label: Density (g/cm³)
inputs.businessContext: Material density used to convert volume to weight.

businessContext_i18n.en: Material density used to convert volume to weight.

rules.message: Part weight, stock volume, and density must all be positive.
Batch size must be at least 1.
Machining time must be greater than 0.
Scrap rate must be between 0% and 50%.

thresholds.message: Scrap rate exceeds 10% — investigate process capability (Cp/Cpk).
Small batch size — setup cost per part may dominate.
Machine rate exceeds $200/hr — verify if high-precision or multi-axis.

formulas.label: Raw Material Cost per Part
formulas.description: Cost of raw material block used for one part, accounting for scrap.

formulas.label: Machining Cost per Part
formulas.description: Cost of machine time per part including setup amortization.

formulas.label: Labor Cost per Part
formulas.description: Direct labor cost per part including setup amortization.

formulas.label: Total Direct Cost per Part
formulas.description: Sum of material, machining, labor, and tooling costs.

formulas.label: Overhead Cost per Part
formulas.description: Allocated overhead based on direct cost.

formulas.label: Total Cost per Part (Primary Result)
formulas.description: Fully burdened cost per good part including scrap allowance.

primary.label: Total Cost per Part
primary.description: Fully burdened cost per good part.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Setup Amortization per Part
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Hidden cost of setup time spread over batch size.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Scrap Loss per Good Part
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional cost due to scrapped parts.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Material Utilization Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost of material removed as chips (not recovered).

suggestedActions.action: Implement SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die) to reduce setup time by 50%.
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Reduces setup amortization per part by up to 50%.

suggestedActions.action: Apply Six Sigma DMAIC to identify root causes of scrap; consider SPC monitoring.
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Reduces scrap rate to below 2%, lowering total cost per part.

suggestedActions.action: Evaluate tool coatings and feeds/speeds to extend tool life; use ISO 13399 tool data.
Combine orders or use group technology to increase batch size.
suggestedActions.expected_impact: Reduces setup cost per part significantly.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Weighted confidence based on input accuracy (0-1). Adjusts final cost by ±5% for uncertainty.
